{{日本の氏族 |家名 = 浮田国造 |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[吉弥侯部|吉弥侯部氏]] |家祖 = [[鹿我別|賀我別王]] |種別 = [[皇別]] |出身地 = [[大和国|大倭国]] |根拠地 = 浮田国
（のちの[[陸奥国]][[宇多郡]]、[[行方郡 (福島県)|行方郡]]） |人物 = [[#人物]]を参照 |支流 = [[針間鴨国造]]
[[上毛野国造]]
[[下毛野国造]] }} '''浮田国造'''（うきたのくにのみやつこ、うきたこくぞう）は、[[#支配領域|浮田国]]（[[陸奥国]]東部）を支配した[[国造]]。 == 概要 == === 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」によれば、[[成務天皇|成務朝]]に[[崇神天皇]]の五世孫の'''[[鹿我別|賀我別王]]'''を国造に定めたとされる。 === 氏族 === '''[[吉弥侯部|吉弥侯部氏]]'''（きみこべうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は不明）で、[[上毛野氏]]、[[下毛野氏]]、[[針間鴨国造]]などと同族。[[神護景雲]]元年（[[767年]]）[[7月19日]]には[[宇多郡]]人の外[[正六位]]上勳十等吉弥侯部石麻呂が'''上毛野陸奥[[キミ (カバネ)|公]]'''を賜姓されている。 == 本拠 == === 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''浮田国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[律令国]]の[[陸奥国]]'''[[宇多郡]]'''、'''[[行方郡 (福島県)|行方郡]]'''、現在の[[福島県]][[相馬市]]、[[南相馬市]]、[[相馬郡]][[新地町]]、同郡[[飯舘村]]に相当する。浮田国造が設置された同時代には、北西部に[[伊久国造]]（[[伊具郡]]）、南部に[[染羽国造]]（[[標葉郡]]）が置かれた。 [[大化の改新]]後に全国的に[[評|評制]]が実施された際、[[宇多川]]流域が宇多評に、[[新田川]]流域が行方評になったと見る説がある[[平川南]]『東北「海道」の古代史』[[岩波書店]]、2012年、122頁。。 == 氏神 == [[宇多郡]]唯一の[[式内社]]で[[名神大社]]の'''[[子眉嶺神社]]'''。 === 墓 === *'''[[桜井古墳]]'''（さくらいこふん） *:[[福島県]][[南相馬市]]にある全長74メートルの[[前方後方墳]]で、[[4世紀]]後半から[[5世紀]]世紀初頭の築造とされる。立地や築造時期から浮田国造の初祖・[[鹿我別|鹿我別命]]の墓と見る説がある[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm 吉弥侯部姓斑目氏の系譜]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。。 *'''[[真野古墳群]]'''（まのこふんぐん） *:福島県南相馬市にある[[古墳時代]]後期の大規模[[古墳群]]で、2基の[[前方後円墳]]と多数の[[円墳]]で構成されている。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[俘囚]] {{DEFAULTSORT:うきたのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:陸奥国]] [[Category:国造]] {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | base = Ukita Province
(later {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the first year of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Sōma, Fukushima|ja|相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and parts of {{ill|Shinchi, Fukushima|ja|新地町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Iitate, Fukushima|ja|飯舘村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the south. During the reforms following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''{{ill|Sakurai Kofun|ja|桜井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' : Located in {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''{{ill|Mano Kofungun|ja|真野古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | base = Ukita Province

The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Sōma, Fukushima|ja|相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and parts of {{ill|Shinchi, Fukushima|ja|新地町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Iitate, Fukushima|ja|飯舘村|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to the south. During the reforms following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''{{ill|Sakurai Kofun|ja|桜井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' : Located in {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''{{ill|Mano Kofungun|ja|真野古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|qq=}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} | base = Ukita Province
(later {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}} {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seimu|qq=}}, appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingō-keiun|qq=}} (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Sōma, Fukushima|ja|相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamisōma|qq=}}, and parts of {{ill|Shinchi, Fukushima|ja|新地町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchi, Fukushima|qq=}} and {{ill|Iitate, Fukushima|ja|飯舘村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitate, Fukushima|qq=}} in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the south. During the reforms following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|qq=}}, the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''{{ill|Sakurai Kofun|ja|桜井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Kofun|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamisōma|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''{{ill|Mano Kofungun|ja|真野古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mano Kofungun|qq=}}''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kimikobe clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]]

{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seimu|qq=}}, appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingō-keiun|qq=}} (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Sōma, Fukushima|ja|相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamisōma|qq=}}, and parts of {{ill|Shinchi, Fukushima|ja|新地町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchi, Fukushima|qq=}} and {{ill|Iitate, Fukushima|ja|飯舘村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitate, Fukushima|qq=}} in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the south. During the reforms following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|qq=}}, the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''{{ill|Sakurai Kofun|ja|桜井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Kofun|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamisōma|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''{{ill|Mano Kofungun|ja|真野古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mano Kofungun|qq=}}''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]]

Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the south. During the reforms following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|qq=}}, the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''{{ill|Sakurai Kofun|ja|桜井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Kofun|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamisōma|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''{{ill|Mano Kofun Cluster|lt=Mano Kofun Cluster|lt=Mano Kofungun|ja|真野古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mano Kofungun|qq=}}''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|qq=}} | origin = {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} | base = Ukita Province
(later {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}} {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Seimu|qq=}}, appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}, as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Jingō-keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingō-keiun|qq=}} (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}, corresponding to modern {{ill|Sōma, Fukushima|ja|相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōma, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamisōma|qq=}}, and parts of {{ill|Shinchi, Fukushima|ja|新地町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinchi, Fukushima|qq=}} and {{ill|Iitate, Fukushima|ja|飯舘村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitate, Fukushima|qq=}} in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the south. During the reforms following the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化の改新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taika Reforms|qq=}}, the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''{{ill|Sakurai Kofun|ja|桜井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Kofun|qq=}}''' : Located in {{ill|Minamisōma|ja|南相馬市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamisōma|qq=}}, Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''{{ill|Mano Kofun Cluster|lt=Mano Kofun Cluster|lt=Mano Kofungun|ja|真野古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mano Kofungun|qq=}}''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]]

In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|qq=}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}} {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of {{ill|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|ja|Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province|2=ja|3=陸奥国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province|qq=}}. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]]

{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}

They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q108487214]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|qq=}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q108487214]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|qq=|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myojin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=|1=Kobimine-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]]

(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myojin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=|1=Kobimine-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:qq]]

During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q108487214]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|qq=|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[sn:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}}
