(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}}

During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q108487214]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|qq=|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|qq=|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|qq=|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|qq=|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|qq=|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}}

[[ja:浮田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q108487214]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}}

[[ja:浮田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q108487214]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:浮田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q108487214]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:浮田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q108487214]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:浮田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q108487214]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}}

It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}}

revision.text:
[[ja:浮田国造]] [[ja:浮田国造]] [[ja:浮田国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q108487214]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko | crest = | crestcaption = | honsei = {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} | founder = {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}} | type = {{ill|Imperial lineage|ja|皇別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial lineage|1=Kōbetsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581000}} | origin = [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | base = Ukita Province
(later [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] {{ill|Uda District, Fukushima|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District, Fukushima|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}) | notable members = See [[#Notable Members]] | branches = {{ill|Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}
{{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}}
{{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|下毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} }} '''Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (浮田国造), also known as '''Ukita Kokuzō''', was the [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]] who governed the ancient territory of [[Ukita Province]], corresponding to the eastern part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. == Overview == === Ancestry === According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' ("Records of Ancient Matters"), the office of Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], appointing '''{{ill|Kagawaki|ja|賀我別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', a fifth-generation descendant of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]], as the first ruler of the region. === Clan === The clan name was '''{{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}}''' (read "Kimikobe Uji"). They were kin to the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and other related clans such as the {{ill|Harima Kamo clan|ja|針間鴨国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Kamo clan|1=Harima Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108487215}}. In the first year of [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō-keiun]] (767 CE), on {{nihongo|July 19|7月19日}}, Kimikobe Ishimaro (吉弥侯部石麻呂), a resident of {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}}, was granted the surname of "Kamitsukeno Mutsu no Kimi" (上毛野陸奥公). == Territory == === Domain === The Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed the region known as "Ukita Province," which later became part of [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]]. The domain encompassed areas in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} and {{ill|Nakama District, Fukushima|ja|行方郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakama District, Fukushima|1=Nakama District, Fukushima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16769301}}, corresponding to modern [[:en:Sōma, Fukushima|Sōma, Fukushima]], [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], and parts of [[:en:Shinchi, Fukushima|Shinchi, Fukushima]] and [[:en:Iitate, Fukushima|Iitate, Fukushima]] in Fukushima Prefecture. Neighboring domains at the time included the {{ill|Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伊久国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Iku no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q85876227}} to the northwest and the {{ill|Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|染羽国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Someha no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the south. During the reforms following the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]], the regions were restructured, with the Uda River valley forming Uda District and the Nitta River valley forming Nakama District.Hirakawa Minami, ''Ancient History of the Tōhoku "Kaido,"'' Iwanami Shoten, 2012, p. 122. == Religious Significance == === Clan Shrine === The sole {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in {{ill|Uda District|ja|宇多郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda District|1=Uda district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449246}} is the '''{{ill|Komidake Shrine|ja|子眉嶺神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komidake Shrine|1=Kobimine Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447922}}''', believed to be associated with the Ukita no Kuni no Miyatsuko. === Kofuns === * '''[[:en:Sakurai Kofun|Sakurai Kofun]]''' : Located in [[:en:Minamisōma|Minamisōma]], Fukushima Prefecture, this keyhole-shaped tumulus (全長74メートル) dates to the late 4th or early 5th century. It is considered the possible burial site of {{ill|Kagawaki|ja|鹿我別命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawaki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿我別|1=Kagawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17226573}}, the clan's progenitor.Hōga Toshio, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/kimikobe1.htm Genealogy of the Kimikobe-surnamed Madara Clan]," 2016. * '''[[:en:Mano Kofun Cluster|Mano Kofungun]]''' : Also located in Minamisōma, this large cluster of Kofuns from the late Kofun period includes two keyhole-shaped tumuli and numerous circular mounds. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Fushū]] * {{ill|Kibiya clan|ja|吉弥侯部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimikobe clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉美侯部}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|浮田国造|version=91695221|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] }} [[ja:浮田国造]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q108487214}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]
